business.headline: GSP Crop Science IPO: should you subscribe to the public listing? Check GMPGSP Crop Science IPO is a book-built issue of Rs 400.00 crore. It includes a fresh issue of 75 lakh shares worth Rs 240.00 crore and an offer for sale of 50 lakh shares worth Rs 160.00 crore.

Should married couples be allowed to file taxes together in India?In most homes, finances are shared. But when it comes to taxes, couples are treated as individuals. Should that change? A new idea is now putting this question in focus.

How goal-based investing can transform savers into long-term wealth-builders?Goal-based investing reframes personal finance from a pursuit of returns into a pursuit of readiness. The most meaningful financial success is not the highest number on a statement, but the confidence that money will be available when life demands it.

Power stocks rise today; Adani Power gains 2% after 1,600 MW deal winThe buying interest was visible across the sector, led by stocks such as Tata Power, NLC India, Torrent Power and Adani Power. Among the top movers, Adani Power remained in focus after a fresh business development.

West Asia conflict triggers global helium shortage. Why it's no laughing matterCrude oil and LNG have been in the limelight amid the ongoing West Asia war. But another critical gas, helium, is now facing a global supply shock and here's why it's bad news.
Sensex, Nifty open flat as crude oil prices weigh in on investor sentimentThe S&P BSE Sensex gained 63.36 points to 75,566.21, while the NSE Nifty50 added 21.90 points to 23,430.70 as of 9:42 am.

Explained: Why gold prices are not rising despite ongoing Iran warIt is unusual for gold to fall during volatile situations like war. But that is exactly what is happening now and it raises a key question about what is driving prices today

Tenant vs landlord: How India's new rental rules change the balanceRenting a home in India has long been a game of negotiation, where rules were often unclear and power rarely equal between tenants and landlords. A new rental framework based on the Model Tenancy Act, 2021 is trying to formalise how homes are rented in India.

Live: Congress stages protest, distributes coal to roadside eateries in BhopalLPG crisis live updates: Amid shortage fears triggered by ongoing conflict in West Asia, fresh supplies are beginning to arrive in India. After the Shivalik vessel reached on Monday, another LPG carrier, Nanda Devi, has now arrived at Gujarat's Vadinar port with over 47,000 metric tonnes, easing immediate pressure. Even so, the impact of earlier disruptions continues to be felt across sectors, with cities like Pune reporting strain and limited cafeteria operations. Authorities have also intensified action against hoarding, with raids, FIRs and arrests reported across states, while the government pushes commercial users to shift to piped natural gas and maintains there is no nationwide shortage.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market fall or rise today?Early signals suggest a positive start. GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,515.50 at around 8:00 am. This points to the Nifty 50 opening above Monday's closing level of 23,408.8.

How young professionals are piling up Rs 10–50 lakh debt through small EMIsIt begins with a small EMI—a new phone, a quick getaway, but for many young professionals, these tiny payments are quietly adding up to tens of lakhs. Are they prepared to handle it?

Gold drops over Rs 3,400, silver cheaper by Rs 11,000: Is it time to invest?Despite global tensions, gold and silver turned cheaper, with gold down Rs 3,400 and silver down Rs 11,000. Does this decline signal a good entry point for investors?

Government urges LPG users to switch to PNG as companies roll out incentivesSujata Sharma, Joint Secretary in the Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas, said the move has been discussed over the past few days as authorities look to ensure stable fuel availability across the country.
Sensex ends 939 points higher, Nifty above 23,300; SBI up 2%The S&P BSE Sensex was up 938.93 points to end at 75,502.85, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 257.70 points to close at 23,408.80.

Saudi Arabia offers alternate Red Sea route as Strait of Hormuz faces crisisSaudi Arabia is now giving its long-term customers the option to receive their April oil allocations through the Red Sea port of Yanbu instead of the usual Persian Gulf terminals.

Citigroup lowers Nifty target to 27,000 amid war risksThe global brokerage has cut its Nifty target to 27,000 from the earlier estimate of 28,500. Even after the reduction, the new target still suggests a possible upside of about 17% from the Nifty's last closing level.

Buying motor insurance? Why the fine print may matter more than the premiumMotor insurance is intended to shift risk from households. Mis-selling this type of insurance has the reverse effect, leaving the consumer vulnerable at the very time they are seeking protection.

PhonePe delays IPO as Iran conflict cools primary marketPhonePe said on Monday that it has temporarily deferred its public market listing due to rising uncertainty in global markets linked to the conflict in West Asia. The company added that it will restart the process once conditions in capital markets become more stable.

Bandhan Bank share price crashes 10%: Why is the stock down today?Bandhan Bank's stock was trading at around Rs 159.50 at about 1.50 pm, down 9.19% on the National Stock Exchange (BSE). Read to know why the shares of the bank tumbled today.

Central Mine Planning IPO: Price set at Rs 172. Worth subscribing as per GMP?The Central Mine Planning IPO is a book-built issue and the entire offer consists of an offer for sale. A total of 10.71 crore shares will be sold in the issue, taking the total size of the IPO to Rs 1,842.12 crore.

entertainment.headline: Ballet and opera have defeated Timothee Chalamet: Jon Stewart Jon Stewart reacted to Timothee Chalamet's remarks about people not caring about ballet and opera any more. He said that the classical arts defeated Chalamet in a "knockout."

BB19's Tanya Mittal thanks Ektaa Kapoor in emotional note: Mumbai feels like home Tanya Mittal shared a video from Ektaa Kapoor's Hoonur launch party, showing the two dancing and enjoying the event. She also posted a note thanking Ektaa for her support and for making Mumbai feel like home.

Nora Fatehi's KD song row: National Human Rights Commission issues notice to team The National Human Rights Commission has issued a notice to the makers of KD - The Devil over alleged use of vulgar and suggestive song lyrics. The controversial track has been removed from social media following public backlash.

Zendaya reacts to viral AI wedding pics with Tom Holland: Many were fooled Zendaya reacted to viral AI-generated wedding photos with Tom Holland, revealing that many people believed they were real and sparking fresh curiosity about the rumours.

Ayushmann Khurrana shares unheard version of Saadi Galli from Nautanki Saala. Video Ayushmann Khurrana shared a never-heard-before verse of Saadi Galli from Nautanki Saala! after being inspired by fan videos on social media.

Just don't tolerate disrespect: Teyana Taylor on security guard row at Oscars Actor Teyana Taylor reacted to the altercation with security at the Academy Awards. In a statement, the Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences also expressed regret over the incident that the actor had to endure.

Nora Fatehi's KD song taken down after allegations of vulgar lyrics The makers of the Kannada film, KD: The Devil, have made the Nora Fatehi song private after widespread allegations of suggestive lyrics and visuals.  The National Human Rights Commission has reportedly issued notices to the filmmakers following backlash.

Busy with film names: Onir's dig at CBFC silence over new Nora Fatehi song Filmmaker Onir questioned the CBFC's priorities amid backlash over Nora Fatehi and Sanjay Dutt's 'Sarke Chunar' lyrics. The row has sparked a wider debate on censorship and content regulation.

Indie film Dihadi makers flag similarities with Homebound: Resemblances are uncanny The makers of the indie film Dihadi have pointed out striking visual similarities with the Neeraj Ghaywan's Homebound, sparking conversations on social media.

After Hema Malini, Esha Deol reacts to Dharmendra missing from Oscars In Memoriam Esha Deol reacted to her father, late actor Dharmendra, being snubbed from the Oscars' In Memoriam segment. The actor said that her father's legacy is beyond this and that he never cared for such honours.

Vishwanath and Sons teaser: Suriya, Mamitha Baiju star in unlikely romance The teaser of Vishwanath and Sons starring Suriya and Mamitha Baiju hints at an unlikely romance and emotional drama.  The film also marks Raveena Tandon's comeback to Tamil cinema after 25 years.

Sean Penn skips Oscars to visit Zelenskyy in war-hit Kyiv after third win Hollywood actor Sean Penn skipped the Oscars after winning Best Supporting Actor to visit Ukrainian President Zelenskyy in war-torn Kyiv, showing his steadfast support for Ukraine amid ongoing conflict.

Dhurandhar 2 report: Rs 123 crore in pre-sales globally, post-midnight shows added Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge is off to a promising start, having raked in a massive Rs 123 crore in global pre-sales.  Reports suggest that multiplexes are scheduling post-midnight shows for the Aditya Dhar film.

Mere Sai writers allege unpaid dues worth lakhs, accuse producers of going silent The writers of the popular 2023 television show Mere Sai complained of unpaid dues by the production house. In an exclusive interview, the writers shared that their payment have been pending for three years.

Besties of The Ton: How Bridgerton redefined friendship goals Beyond the sweeping romances, Bridgerton's latest season celebrates the friendships that truly matter. From Sophie and Alfie's loyal bond to the unbreakable ties among the ton's matriarchs, these relationships stand just as strong as any love story.

BTS: The Return trailer reaffirms long-awaited comeback as a global cultural reset Pop sensation BTS unveiled the trailer for BTS: The Return, highlighting their emotional journey after four years. The documentary is centred on reunion and resilience, featuring the traditional Korean folk song Arirang.

Dune 3: Zendaya-Timothee return, Robert Pattinson joins cast; see new posters The posters of Dune: Part 3 are out. The new instalment marks the entry of Robert Pattinson, who is believed to be playing the villain.

Chiraiya asks the uncomfortable marital rape question: Does marriage cancel consent? In Chiraiya, a small-town woman begins to question the rules she has lived by all her life. As Divya Dutta's Kamles confronts uncomfortable truths, the show asks a difficult question: does marriage erase consent?

Bruno Mars dismisses claims of shading Taylor Swift: Only love for her Singer Bruno Mars reacted to accidentally liking a reel on Instagram that called Taylor Swift "talentless." In a social media post, he praised the singer and wrote how much love and respect he holds for her.

general news.headline: Dressing up massacre as military op: India slams Pak for Kabul hospital attackThe strike on a drug rehabilitation hospital in Kabul, which killed over 400 and left nearly 300 injured, comes amid a sharp escalation in the conflict between Pakistan and Afghanistan.

Age limit for maternity benefit unconstitutional: SC shields women adopting child older than 3 monthsAge limit for maternity benefit unconstitutional: SC shields women adopting child older than 3 months

High Court orders takedown of posts linking Hardeep Puri's daughter to EpsteinDelhi HC grants interim relief to Himayani Puri, orders removal of posts linking her to Epstein, seeks replies from platforms and defendants within two weeks

What have you done, friend shouts after filming Delhi man shoot himselfThe man loaded the magazine, cocked the pistol, briefly looked at the camera, and then shot himself in the left side of his chest.

You Indian? Get lost: Are Indians treated badly in Vietnam?Vietnam has grown popular among Indian travellers, but some say they faced hostility in shops and markets. Tourists and locals suggest stereotypes about Indians may be contributing to the friction.

Live: Missile debris kills Pak national in Abu Dhabi; blasts in Doha, DubaiUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The Middle East conflict continues to escalate with fresh developments across the region. US President Donald Trump said Washington was "shocked" after Iran began striking Gulf countries including Kuwait and Bahrain, widening the war's scope. Meanwhile, tensions have intensified around the Strait of Hormuz as several US allies declined Trump's call to deploy warships to escort oil tankers. Israel also reported new Iranian missile launches, while global markets remain on edge amid fears of prolonged disruption to energy supplies. Follow indiatoday.in for the latest live updates.

Where is Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu?Over the past week, social media has been abuzz with rumours claiming that Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu was killed in an Iranian missile strike. However, there remains no credible evidence. It coincided with his reported absence from cabinet meetings. However, Israel has released videos and images to assert that Netanyahu is alive.

Live: Was like doomsday, says survivor as Pak strike on Kabul rehab kills 408Pakistan-Afghanistan Conflict Live Updates: At least 400 people were killed and 250 others injured in an airstrike allegedly carried out by Pakistan on a drug rehabilitation hospital in Kabul, the Afghan Taliban government said on Tuesday.

Pakistan, however, rejected the allegation as "false and misleading", maintaining that its strikes on Monday night were precise and targeted only military installations and terrorist support infrastructure. Stay tuned to India Today for the latest updates on the Pakistan-Afghanistan conflict.

Renault Duster returns to India, price starts from Rs 10.49 lakhRenault Duster returns to India with an all-new generation featuring updated design, petrol and hybrid powertrains, advanced technology and ADAS, with prices starting from Rs 10.49 lakh (ex-showroom).

Indians need 134 years to get Green Card, Pakistanis just 2: Immigration expertAn Indian-origin immigration advocate has claimed that Indians are forced to stay on the H-1B visa due to systematic faults within the green card system. His comments come amid a backlog of Indians waiting for employment-based green cards that is now estimated to take at least 134 years to clear. Meanwhile, for Pakistan and Somalia, the wait is two years or less.

We don't need anybody: Trump fumes as allies snub call to join Hormuz missionMany of the United States' key allies have rejected President Donald Trump's call to deploy warships to the Strait of Hormuz to reopen the strategic oil route blocked by Iran.

People follow tiger for pictures near Tadoba, video triggers massive outrageA video from an area near the Tadoba Tiger Reserve in Maharashtra shows people dangerously crowding around a tiger to take pictures of the wild animal.

Is oral sex replacing smoking as the leading cause of throat cancer?While smoking remains the leading cause of throat cancer in India, HPV infections transmitted through oral sex have overtaken tobacco as the primary cause in countries such as the US and the UK.

Trump stunned by intelligence claim that Mojtaba Khamenei may be gay: ReportAccording to a report by the New York Post, US intelligence suggested Mojtaba may have had a long-term relationship with a man who served as his childhood tutor. The news outlet said the claim was revealed to it by two intelligence community officials and a person close to the White House.

After the Shivalik vessel reached on Monday, another LPG carrier, Nanda Devi, has now arrived at Gujarat's Vadinar port with over 47,000 metric tonnes, easing immediate pressure. Even so, the impact of earlier disruptions continues to be felt across sectors, with cities like Pune reporting strain and limited cafeteria operations. Authorities have also intensified action against hoarding, with raids, FIRs and arrests reported across states, while the government pushes commercial users to shift to piped natural gas and maintains there is no nationwide shortage.

Trump says nobody expected Iran's Gulf attacks. Experts had warned him beforehandTrump claimed Iran's retaliatory strikes on Gulf nations were unexpected. But intelligence reports and experts had warned in advance that Tehran could escalate by targeting regional countries.

This Mumbai auto driver's daily earnings left a BTech graduate speechlessA Mumbai auto driver's claim of earning Rs 2,500 per day has sparked a social media discussion about income vs social recognition and the realities of different professions.

Norwegian Ambassador to India travels in Delhi Metro for the first time, shares picsNorwegian Ambassador May-Elin Stener shared pictures of her travelling on the Delhi Metro for the first time in a now-viral post on social media.

After Shivalik, LPG tanker Nanda Devi arrives at Gujarat port from Hormuz straitYesterday, Shivalik, the other LPG tanker, arrived in Gujarat's Mundra Port with over 46,000 metric tons of liquefied petroleum gas. The vessel is expected to meet nearly one day of India's total LPG import requirement.

Should a seven-year-old hit the gym?Children naturally mirror their parents. But as millennial and Gen Z parents make the gym a part of everyday life, should their kids follow suit? We leave it for experts to answer.

sports.headline: FIFA World Cup to suffer same fate as cricket? Iran urges games to be moved out of USA Iran's football federation is pushing to move its 2026 World Cup matches from the US to Mexico over safety fears, in a controversy drawing comparisons to Bangladesh's T20 World Cup crisis.

Tilak Varma felicitated by Telangana CM Revanth Reddy after T20 World Cup triumph India T20 World Cup winner Tilak Varma received a warm welcome in his home state after the tournament triumph. Telangana Chief Minister A Revanth Reddy felicitated the young batter for his role in India's historic title defence.

Is CSK forcing a spot for MS Dhoni? De Villiers puzzled by veteran's role in IPL 2026 IPL 2026: AB de Villiers questions MS Dhoni's role at CSK in IPL 2026, saying the legend must bat higher or risk playing for the wrong reasons as Sanju Samson's arrival reshapes Chennai's lineup.

IPL as big as international cricket: Ricky Ponting explains India's huge talent pool Ricky Ponting believes the Indian Premier League plays a major role in shaping India's T20 success. The former Australia captain said IPL matches are as intense as international games and help prepare players for the national team.

Neymar focused on FIFA World Cup dream despite another Brazil squad snub Neymar's hopes of playing at the 2026 FIFA World Cup remain alive despite another Brazil squad snub. The Santos forward admitted he was disappointed after being left out of Brazil's upcoming friendlies in the United States.

Watch: Rohit Sharma in top gear in MI nets session ahead of IPL 2026 IPL 2026: Rohit Sharma has already started turning heads ahead of IPL 2026. A viral video from Mumbai Indians' training session showed the former India captain going after the bowlers, raising excitement among fans before the new season.

A war crime: Afghanistan cricketers slam deadly Pak strikes on Kabul hospital Afghanistan cricket stars Rashid Khan and Mohammad Nabi have strongly condemned reported Pakistani airstrikes in Kabul. The two players expressed grief and anger over civilian casualties after a hospital was reportedly struck during the attack.

KKR's fast bowling problem: 2024 winners head towards shaky start to IPL campaign Kolkata Knight Riders enter the new IPL season with a fast-bowling unit already battered by injuries and uncertainty. With Harshit Rana unlikely to feature and Matheesha Pathirana still recovering, the defending champions suddenly look short of pace options and experience.

Pratika Rawal opens up on her deep bond with Test cricket: This is my format Pratika Rawal said that she considers Test cricket the ultimate measure of a cricketer's character, patience, and determination. She made her debut in the Day-Night Perth Test against Australia, scoring her maiden fifty.

Chennai Super Kings appoint ex-England wicketkeeper as fielding coach for IPL 2026 Chennai Super Kings have appointed former England wicketkeeper-batter James Foster as their new fielding coach ahead of IPL 2026, adding an experienced coach to Stephen Fleming's support staff.

Liam Livingstone slams Brendon McCullum, Rob Key in sensational rant against ECBLiam Livingstone launched a blistering attack on England's team management, accusing Brendon McCullum and Rob Key of poor communication and a lack of support after being dropped from the white-ball setup.

The story of how Suryakumar, not Hardik Pandya, became India's T20I captainSuryakumar Yadav was made the T20I captain after the 2024 World Cup, pipping the then vice-captain Hardik Pandya. The batter reveals how he got to know about the news.

Vaishnavi Adkar continues dream run, closes in on India's No.1 WTA rankingVaishnavi Adkar's rapid rise continued as she broke into the WTA top 400 for the first time, climbing to a career-best No. 397 after winning the ITF W35 Kalaburagi title and moving within six spots of India's No.1 ranking.

Pakistan fast bowler grabs limelight with massive no-ball in National T20 CupPakistan pacer Asad Akhtar grabbed attention after bowling a huge no-ball during Karachi Blues' National T20 Cup clash against Lahore Blues on Saturday at the Imran Khan Cricket Stadium in Peshawar.

Chelsea fined 131 crore rupees, 1-year-ban for breaching financial rulesChelsea have been fined $14.3 million and handed a suspended transfer ban after the Premier League found the club guilty of breaching financial regulations. Undisclosed payments, made between 2011 and 2018 have landed the club in trouble.

It is not Shubman Gill: Fans slam BCCI over huge mistake in Naman AwardsThe Indian Cricket Board is being called out for an extremely silly mistake at the Naman Awards 2026. During the ceremony, BCCI credited Shubman Gill as the captain who won the Champions Trophy, and not Rohit Sharma.

Antonelli arrives, Hamilton makes comeback in scintillating Shanghai showdownShanghai GP: Kimi Antonelli's maiden win, Lewis Hamilton's Ferrari debut podium, and Max Verstappen's frustration headlined a thrilling Chinese Grand Prix as Formula 1's 2026 season bursts into life in Shanghai.

MS Dhoni always has a positive influence on me: Sanju Samson eager for CSK stintSanju Samson said he is excited to share the CSK dressing room with MS Dhoni in IPL 2026, adding that the former India captain has always had a positive influence on him and will be a valuable source of learning.

Unleash the real Hardik Pandya: Harbhajan Singh on what MI needs to win IPL 2026IPL 2026: Harbhajan Singh believes that the Mumbai Indians need to find a way to bring out the best in Hardik Pandya should they stand a chance of getting back to winning titles.

Ricky Ponting explains the bold call that shaped India's T20 WC title defenceRicky Ponting said India's bold call to restore Sanju Samson as opener and move Ishan Kishan to No. 3 played a major role in their successful T20 World Cup 2026 title defence.

Fastest IPL hundred or Chris Gayle's 175? Vaibhav Sooryavanshi makes his pickDuring the Naman Awards on Sunday, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi revealed that he aspires to break Chris Gayle's long-standing record of 175, the highest individual score in T20 cricket.

Buying Pak players contributes to death of Indian soldiers: GavaskarSunil Gavaskar has criticised SunRisers Leeds owners for signing Pakistan spinner Abrar Ahmed at The Hundred 2026 auction, arguing that Indian owners must not contribute to Pakistani government revenues.

No India T20 World Cup cricketer nominated for February's Player of the Month awardDespite India's successful T20 World Cup title defence, no Indian men's cricketer was shortlisted for the ICC Men's Player of the Month award for February. But India have representation in the women's category, with pacer Arundhati Reddy nominated.

India can field two playing XIs: Suryakumar Yadav hails country's talent poolIndia captain Suryakumar Yadav believes the country's T20 talent pool has grown so deep that multiple international-quality teams could be formed. He credits domestic cricket and the IPL for building India's remarkable strength in the format.

Was Gautam Gambhir behind Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma's Test retirements?Was Gautam Gambhir behind the Test retirements of Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma? India head coach Gautam Gambhir has hinted at the possible reason while speaking in an event in Kolkata.

India dominating cricket on all fronts: Mithali Raj calls it a dream come trueFormer India captain Mithali Raj believes Indian cricket is finally living up to its long-held potential. With the men's, women's and junior teams winning global titles, she says the country is witnessing a proud era of dominance.

Karnataka government clears Chinnaswamy stadium to host IPL 2026 matchesIPL 2026: Karnataka government has approved IPL matches at Chinnaswamy Stadium after an expert committee reviewed safety and preparedness measures at a meeting chaired by Home Minister G. Parameshwara.

Who is Max Dowman? Arsenal's wonderkid now Premier League's youngest scorerArsenal teenager Max Dowman made Premier League history with a stunning goal against Everton. At just 16 years and 73 days, the winger became the youngest goalscorer the league has ever seen.

Can Carrick bring the good times back to Manchester United? Rooney shares his stanceSeven wins from nine games have taken Manchester United from sixth to third under Michael Carrick, with Wayne Rooney and fans growing louder in their calls for him to be handed the job permanently.

Tried to make Gambhir laugh many times during T20 World Cup, reveals Suryakumar YadavSuryakumar Yadav revealed India's players tried repeatedly to make coach Gautam Gambhir laugh during the T20 World Cup 2026, finally succeeding after their title win in Ahmedabad on Sunday night.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
